title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · Compiler Case: Ambiguous Publish Recovery
summary: A producer opens a normal channel, calls waitForConfirms after every message, discards sequence identity, and blindly republishes all work after a connection loss even when the broker may already own it. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ambiguous publish recovery increment in confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify unresolved confirms after failure as ambiguous, retain stable message identity, and republish only through a duplicate-safe downstream contract.
Explain when confirms occur for unroutable messages, persistent classic queues, quorum queues, and multiple routed queues.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Ambiguous Publish Recovery · Theory Ambiguous Publish Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 2006 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify unresolved confirms after failure as ambiguous, retain stable message identity, and republish only through a duplicate-safe downstream contract.
